//include-component-in-tag: false → every linked package tags at ONE shared, component-less tag (vX.Y.Z) and cuts ONE aggregated GitHub Release, instead of six per-component tags/releases. This is the idiomatic release-please lockstep-monorepo setup (NOT skip-github-release, which suppresses the anchoring tag and breaks the next run — see the 0.3.1 recovery in DECISIONS/PR history).
//draft: Cut ONE aggregated GitHub Release (component-less, tag vX.Y.Z) as a DRAFT; the release workflow (finalize-release) flips it to public only after all builds + tests + registry publishes succeed (announcement integrity). force-tag-creation is REQUIRED with draft: GitHub does not create the git tag for a draft release, and without the tag release-please cannot find the prior release on the next run (wrong changelog base). See release.yml finalize-release.

Root aggregate. release-type 'simple' + path '.' → this package is assigned ALL commits (release-please special-cases '.'), so its CHANGELOG.md and release notes are the UNION of every package's changes. Listed FIRST so it is created first and WINS the shared component-less tag vX.Y.Z: its union notes become the ONE aggregated GitHub Release body (the 6 per-package releases then collide on the same tag and are skipped as harmless duplicates — verified non-fatal in release-please source). No version.txt: 'simple' updates it with createIfMissing:false, so its absence is a skipped warning, not an error; the version lives in the manifest.
